items.title: Criminal Trial Lawyers' Association calls for criminal probe into Edmonton police chief, lawyer
items.summary_auto: The Criminal Trial Lawyers' Association says serious questions surround Chief Warren Driechel and police lawyer Megan Hankewich for their statements about a recent manslaughter case involving the death of an eight-year-old girl named Nina Napope.

items.title: 'Organized chaos' of Rock League strategy keeping curlers on their toes in early going
items.summary_auto: Rock League has not been perfect through two days. But if the goal of this one-week Rock League preview season is proof of concept, then you can see how it all might come together.

items.title: Why Canada's reaction to Israel's invasion of Lebanon is so different this time
items.summary_auto: Canada's response to Israel's 2026 invasion of Lebanon has been unequivocal condemnation of what Prime Minister Mark Carney called "an illegal invasion." That's a marked contrast to Stephen Harper's defence of Israel's 2006 invasion.

items.title: Blue Jays' Addison Barger put on 10-day injured list
items.summary_auto: Blue Jays utility player Addison Barger has been put on the 10-day injured list, further testing the Toronto ball club's ability to withstand the multitude of injuries that have been piling up in the early days of the season.

items.title: Ukrainians find glimmer of hope in war time by releasing hundreds of bats into the wild
items.summary_auto: Over the weekend more than 1,000 spectators gathered in Ukraine to carefully open cloth bags and release dozens of rescued bats into the air. Alona Pryslutska, co-founder of the Ukrainian Bat Rehab Center, says not even the war is stopping this unconventional marker of the arrival of spring.

items.title: Rory McIlroy gets morning start to Masters title defence
items.summary_auto: Rory McIlroy will launch his Masters title defence Thursday alongside world No. 3 Cameron Young and amateur Mason Howell.

items.title: Disgraced former Manitoba law school dean disbarred in U.K. over 'blatant and sustained dishonesty'
items.summary_auto: Former University of Manitoba law school dean Jonathan Black-Branch, who was disbarred in Manitoba in 2024, has now also been disbarred in England and Wales.

items.title: Top Canadian commander in Latvia temporarily removed following compensation probe
items.summary_auto: Canada's military operations commander has temporarily removed the officer in charge of the country's biggest overseas deployment. The decision by Lt.-Gen. Steve Boivin comes after Col. James Smith was charged by military police with prejudice to good order regarding compensation irregularities while he commanded Task Force Latvia.

items.title: Sask. volunteers spent $39K to save their homes from wildfire. Will they be compensated?
items.summary_auto: Wadin Bay, Sask., residents defied a mandatory evacuation order and saved their community from the summer fires of 2025. Attempts to recover the firefighting costs have stalled. They wonder how small communities can protect themselves as wildfires increase in the north.

items.title: Carney breaks down plans to spend $51B on local infrastructure over a decade
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney says the federal government has a plan to address infrastructure gaps across Canada with billions of dollars in funding over the coming decade.

items.title: Conservatives call for ethics probe of Champagne as questions raised over high-speed rail
items.summary_auto: The Conservatives are calling for an ethics investigation into Finance Minister François-Philippe Champagne in regards to the involvement of his partner in the Alto high-speed rail project. Prime Minister Mark Carney defended Champagne, saying the minister followed all of the ethics rules.

items.title: 'You can't': Dan Levy says there's no Schitt's Creek sequel without Catherine O'Hara
items.summary_auto: Dan Levy struggled to find the words as he set foot in the Ontario town where the smash hit Schitt's Creek was filmed for the first time since the show wrapped.

items.title: Group denied federal funding for search at former Ontario residential school
items.summary_auto: A group that’s been searching for missing children and unmarked graves at the former McIntosh Indian Residential School in northwestern Ontario says it’s been denied federal funding to continue its work. Here's what we know about the Wiikwogaming Tiinahtiisiiwin project and why its funding hasn't been renewed.

items.title: Grassy Narrows First Nation denied federal funding for search for unmarked graves at former residential school
items.summary_auto: A group that’s been searching for missing children and unmarked graves at the former McIntosh Indian Residential School in northwestern Ontario says it’s been denied federal funding to continue its work. Here's what we know about the Wiikwogaming Tiinahtiisiiwin project and why its funding hasn't been renewed.

items.title: Artemis II astronauts capture stunning 'Earthset' photo
items.summary_auto: A day after the historic lunar flyaround, NASA on Tuesday released striking photos taken by the U.S.-Canadian crew.

items.title: 3 months after the draw, $80M Ontario lotto winners have stepped forward. Here's why a delay isn't unusual
items.summary_auto: More than three months after the winning ticket for a record-setting $80-million Lotto Max win was sold in London, Ont., the massive jackpot is in the process of being claimed. Why did it take so long?

items.title: U.K. blocks Ye, formerly known as Kanye West, from entering the country
items.summary_auto: Organizers for a music festival in London have cancelled the upcoming three-day event after the rapper Ye, who was slated to perform, has been barred from entering the U.K. following past antisemitic remarks. The UK Home Office told CBC News that Ye's permission to travel was blocked because his presence would not be conducive to the public good.

items.title: London music fest cancelled after U.K. bars headliner Ye following past antisemitic remarks
items.summary_auto: Organizers for a music festival in London have cancelled the upcoming three-day event after the rapper Ye, who was slated to perform, has been barred from entering the U.K. following past antisemitic remarks. The UK Home Office told CBC News that Ye's permission to travel was blocked because his presence would not be conducive to the public good.

items.title: Window to watch auroras in southern Canada closing as solar activity declines
items.summary_auto: Southern Canadians have been occasionally treated to neon-soaked night skies of purples, pinks and greens during a peak in the sun’s activity over the last two years. But the celestial light show put on by the northern lights will dim as the sun moves into a quieter phase of its 11-year cycle.

items.title: Decorated Australian soldier Ben Roberts-Smith charged with killing 5 unarmed Afghans during war
items.summary_auto: Australia's most decorated living veteran, Ben Roberts-Smith, faces war crime charges on allegations he killed five unarmed Afghans while serving in Afghanistan from 2009 and 2012, police and media reported on Tuesday.

items.title: British media coverage of flight stuck on 'freezing Canadian island' gets warm laughs in Newfoundland
items.summary_auto: Newfoundlanders were having a chuckle on Monday over international media reports saying passengers on a British Airways flight diverted to St. John's last week were stranded on a frozen island somewhere in Canada.

items.title: Michigan muscles its way to program's 2nd national title, beating stubborn UConn 69-63
items.summary_auto: Michigan's new Fab Five threw style points out the door and brought home a prize not even the school's most famous team could capture.

items.title: LG Energy Solution flags 1st quarter operating loss on weak EV demand
items.summary_auto: LGES, which supplies Tesla, General ​Motors and Hyundai Motor among others, has been grappling with weaker EV battery demand, with one of its ​major customers, GM, idling a Detroit EV plant until ⁠April .

items.title: Rapper Offset in stable condition after shooting in Florida, says spokesperson
items.summary_auto: The rapper Offset, a former member of the influential hip-hop trio Migos, was shot outside a Florida casino and is in stable condition, a spokesperson said Tuesday.

items.title: Gunman near Israeli Consulate in Istanbul killed, Turkish governor says
items.summary_auto: Three assailants opened fire at police outside a building housing the Israeli Consulate in Istanbul on Tuesday, sparking a gunfight that left one attacker dead, Turkish officials said. The two other assailants were captured with injuries.

items.title: Federal fund for organizations cleaning up ocean debris in Maritimes returns with less money
items.summary_auto: ​A federal fund for organizations that do marine and beach cleanups is returning, but with less money behind it than in past years.

items.title: Federal Ghost Gear Fund to return, bringing hope to some organizations in the Maritimes
items.summary_auto: ​A federal fund for organizations that do marine and beach cleanups is returning, but with less money behind it than in past years.

items.title: 'Food is medicine': New Glasgow health clinic hands out veggies to patients
items.summary_auto: The Highland Health Home and Learning Centre in New Glasgow is testing the idea that prevention is cure as it hands out free bags of greens to its patients as part of a 12-week pilot program.

items.title: Trump agrees to 2-week ceasefire as Iran says it will allow passage through Strait of Hormuz
items.summary_auto: U.S. ‌President ​Donald  Trump said ​early Tuesday evening that he ​has ⁠agreed "to ⁠suspend ‌the bombing ⁠and attack on ‌Iran for a ​period ⁠of ⁠two ⁠weeks" subject to Iran reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Psychedelics helped him deal with terminal illness anxiety, but getting them was a long, strange trip
items.summary_auto: Five years after Pete Pearson, 75, was diagnosed with a terminal lung condition, he was experiencing debilitating anxiety and depression known as "end-of-life distress." He wanted to try psilocybin — commonly known as magic mushrooms — to see if it helped. After a lengthy and futile attempt to gain access to the drug legally through Health Canada, Pearson decided this was a trip he needed to take no matter what.

items.title: Why Montreal isn't hosting the World Cup — and FIFA's rigid rules for Toronto, Vancouver
items.summary_auto: Thousands of documents obtained by Radio-Canada's investigative program Enquête shed light on why Montreal isn't among the host cities, how much the event will cost Canadian taxpayers — about $1 billion for 13 games — and the strict conditions the cities of Toronto and Vancouver have to respect.

items.title: How financially healthy is your hospital? Eastern Ontario data reveals a grim picture
items.summary_auto: A CBC News analysis of hospital financial statements reveals many of eastern Ontario's hospitals have been struggling with their bottom line over the past three years, with about half operating in the red and some borrowing millions from banks. Despite a recent funding boost from the province, advocates and experts worry the situation won't improve.

items.title: Doctor says diagnosis of Ontario woman was 'wrong' but defends treatment 1 day before she died in hospital
items.summary_auto: The physician who treated Heather Winterstein the day before she died in hospital from a severe reaction to bacterial infection said he may have erred in diagnosing her condition as being due to "social issues." She was discharged and died the next day, after she returned to hospital.

items.title: Still have to file your taxes? Here's how to find yourself some money
items.summary_auto: Tick, tick, tick. This year's tax deadline is coming quick. Here's what tax experts who spoke with CBC News say you should keep in mind as you're filling out your return so you don't miss out on opportunities to find and save money.

items.title: The Ambassador Bridge has lost its spot as the busiest U.S.-Canada trade corridor
items.summary_auto: High tolls at Windsor's Ambassador Bridge are pushing commercial truck traffic north to Sarnia, where the Blue Water Bridge has become the busiest crossing on the Canada-U.S. border. The shift comes as the delayed, overbudget Gordie Howe bridge opening remains in limbo.

items.title: How Canadian military members violated intelligence-gathering rules during COVID-19
items.summary_auto: Canadian Armed Forces members used their own personal social media accounts, computers and networks at home during the COVID-19 pandemic and gathered information about Canadians, violating intelligence-gathering rules, according to a newly released report.

items.title: Watchdog's report on controversial RCMP unit delayed due to lack of chairperson
items.summary_auto: A years-long investigation into a special RCMP unit that polices protests against resource extraction in British Columbia is finished but can’t be finalized because the RCMP's oversight body has been without a chairperson for more than a year.
